By the way, consider using Local Folders. I find them to be indispensable. For example, suppose you discontinue an email address (or it gets taken away) and online access disappears. creator: Will1946

content:
... but want to populate these from 'All Address Books' where the majority of my address book is to be found.

As you can see from Lin's animation, a mailing list is tied to a specific address book. Hence you need to populate a mailing list from the address book it was created in. 'All Address Books' is literally what the name suggests - a link showing contacts from all address books. However, it isn't an address book itself. I.e. you cannot create a mailing list tied to 'All Address Books' .

Note, if you use Gmail, and you created an online address book for your Google Contacts, you cannot create a mailing list tied to that address book either.

Can you tell me what the 'Collected Addresses' address book is?

The Collected Addresses address book is a built-in, automated repository designed to catch and save contact details on the fly.

By default, every time you send an email to a recipient whose email address isn't already stored in your main Personal Address Book, Thunderbird automatically extracts their name and email address and saves it into Collected Addresses.

It ensures that if you ever need to email that person again, their address will automatically populate in the composition window via auto-complete - even if you never explicitly saved them as a contact.

content:
It's possible (but not easy) to put a contact into an address book by accident if you were fumbling the keys or mouse in a certain order. When the message pane is in focus and you click on the From address, or you were to Tab over to it and it Enter, you get a context menu, one of the choices being "Add to Address Book". I actually never knew this until just today when I found a web site that instructs blind folks how to use Thunderbird!

I must say that I don't wonder that people find Thunderbird's Address Book confusing. It has some limitations that I suppose are due to valid technical reasons, but that doesn't make it any less frustrating. It can do most of the things you would expect an address book to do, but I find that I have to force myself to think like it does rather than the other way around.

There is a help article on this site about Address Books and Mailing Lists, but it is very out-of-date and has some inaccurate information and references to interface elements that no longer exist.

content:
You must be doing something different that I am, or there's something peculiar about your laptop. The attached animations shows me moving the window all over the screen and even offscreen. However, it turns out that this feature of Windows has a quirk: after you use Alt+Space, M, arrow keys to move the window, then hit Esc or Enter to stop moving, if you've moved the windows upwards, it snaps back down. If you have moved it left or right or down, it stays put, but up it snaps back. That makes it not so useful for getting at buttons along the bottom of the window.

Another way to get at the buttons is using the Tab key. Tab will cycle you through all the controls in order, including eventually the OK button. Shift+Tab will take you backwards.

content:
Hello there


Are your server settings correct? Specifically, check that your incoming port is set to 995 and that you are using SSL/TLS encryption for Yahoo.Are you using third-party antivirus software on your PC?

If you encounter any new error messages, please share them with us.


Troubleshoot mode Try enabling troubleshoot mode to temporarily disable security tools, and test toggling your network connection on and off. (Are you connected via wireless or a wired network?). Once the connection is stable, try downloading your emails again. Sudden server timeouts can sometimes resolve on their own once a stable connection is established. Or waiting is the solution.


Big load You mentioned that 1 to 36 messages are set to download at once; this is a large batch. We should check your antivirus software or VPN to see if a 'mail-shield' or aggressive email scanning is enabled. Try turning these off temporarily to see if your emails will download. If you can’t use troubleshooting mode.


Slow Internet If your internet connection is slow, your email client might be timing out too quickly, which is likely causing the errors you're experiencing. How much devices do you use?


Troubleshoot information If nothing changes after that, please send me the troubleshooting information. At the top right of the Thunderbird window, click the menu button and go to Help > Troubleshooting Information. Click the Copy text to clipboard button and paste the new information into your reply to us here


Use https://support.mozilla.org/en-US/kb/troubleshoot-mode-thunderbird

https://www.thunderbird.net/en-GB/download/


Note Please verify if the Yahoo account is configured with OAuth 2.0. Using this modern authentication protocol may resolve the delays you are experiencing when downloading emails.

Is your Thunderbird up to date? Try updating Thunderbird to the latest version, as it might solve the issue described above.
creator: Googlethunderbird.

content:
Check out the Identities feature. It lets you save certain aspects of your messages, including signatures, as "Identities". Then, when you create a message or a response, you can choose from a drop-down list of the ones you have created. Here's an article about them:

https://support.mozilla.org/en-US/kb/using-identities

And here's an article about how to create and use Signatures.

https://support.mozilla.org/en-US/kb/signatures


By the way, any donation you might like to make would be appreciated and would help the project, but it's not expected. Thunderbird is free, open-source software, and there's no money needed to download, install, or use it.

content:
As I understand it, you had a backup of the profile, but when restoring, it would not work. Knowing the problem at the time, may have been useful. But that's history now. You deleted thousands of files related to thunderbird, but did not explain from where. That also may have been useful. So, you have a profile now with the restored addressbook, but not the messages. Here is my suggestion:

  1. set up the POP accounts, but keep offline. That is, do not allow accounts to retrieve mail.
  2. now, exit thunterbird and copy the backup of the profile\Mail folder to overlay whatever is there. The intent here is to keep the same popstate.dat intact so that, when you next retrieve mail, you will pick up from where you left off. On a restart, that should work. BUT, since we don't know why the original restore didn't work, I can't be sure.

If those suggested steps seem inappropriate, then more information is needed. Posting a screenshot of the Mail folder may prove helpful. Thank you.

NOTE: Have you considered using multiple profiles to allow use of multiple drives to minimize loss or backup/restore exposure? Not knowing your details, 280 gig is a lot of gig. :)

First, I believe thunderbird to be stupid, and I say that as a compliment. It does not make decisions, but acts on what we users present to it. When you rightclick on unified folders and click properties and then click 'choose', do the other accounts appear in the list? If so, try putting tickmarks by those accounts. If you do not see those other accounts listed, then try this:

  1. click help>troubleshootinginformation
  2. scroll to 'profile folder' and click 'open folder'
  3. now, exit thunderbird
  4. you are now in the profile, click the Mail folder and you should see 'Smart Mailboxes' folder. Delete it.
  5. restart thunderbird and it will recreate the smart mailboxes automatically. Now, try again to set the accounts by rightclicking the unified folder, clicking properties and then clicking 'choose'

content:
Hello there Nasshorn


I have read your message. I understand you are having an issue with a frozen folder



Troubleshoot mode Everything synced in Thunderbird can also be accessed via the Gmail web interface, where you can read your urgent emails without interruption.Please try launching Thunderbird in Troubleshoot Mode (Safe Mode).

Wrong location If the software functions correctly in this mode, the issue is likely caused by a faulty add-on or extension. If Thunderbird starts in Troubleshoot Mode but still freezes or hangs on a specific folder, the folder's index file might be corrupted (causing the client to look for data in the wrong location). .

Repair folder You can resolve this by using the built-in folder repair tool. Right-click the affected folder, select Properties, and click Repair Folder.


Use https://support.mozilla.org/en-US/kb/troubleshoot-mode-thunderbird

https://support.mozilla.org/en-US/kb/how-to-make-screenshots

If you have any other related issues or error messages, please send us a screenshot.

content:
Account Hub is intended for setting up new accounts. If you already have it set up, working or not, that shouldn't be necessary. But the first screen shot might provide a clue with the message "Can't find a server". That implies to me that there is a network problem between you and them.

Have you confirmed the correct server settings, in case you have to configure them manually? I found this web page, but it seems a little vague to me.

https://www.videotron.com/en/support/internet/email/pop-imap#server-address-informations

Try turning off the "experimental" Account Hub feature (see <Settings | General | Account Hub> and use the old setup routine, and resort to Manual Configuration if necessary.

Do you use a VPN? If so, turn it off, at least temporarily, and try to authenticate again.
